<START_OF_CONTENT(as it is including trailing newlines)># 2026-07 Contract

## Project: autodoc-hero-clone

### Workers
- builder: minimax-MiniMax-M3 (browser-use)
- evaluator: minimax-MiniMax-M3 (browser-use)

---

## Task: Clone autodoc.es above-the-fold vehicle search selector

### User Goal (verbatim)
> Clone the main above-the-fold header section of https://www.autodoc.es/, specifically isolating the complete interactive vehicle search selector component (the multi-tab interface for selecting Passenger Cars, Trucks, Motorcycles, etc., by make, model, and engine). Save the full component inside './autodoc-clone'. It must be functionally and visually identical down to the pixel level, replicating all layout structures, dynamic dropdown behaviors, fonts, exact color tokens, hover states, animations, and any embedded media or layout effects.

### Scope
- **IN:** Above-the-fold header section ONLY — site header/nav (with logo, top utility links/cart) + the interactive vehicle search selector hero (tabs + make/model/engine dropdowns + search button + decorative background/media).
- **OUT:** Below-the-fold sections (promo strip, category tiles, footer, newsletter, app banners). Do NOT clone.

### Deliverables
- `./autodoc-clone/` — full project (Vite + React + Tailwind)
- `npm run dev` must serve a working selector
- `npm run build` must succeed
- Extracted design tokens sheet at `clone-web/team-log/tokens.md`
- Builder plan: `clone-web/team-log/builder_plan.md`
- Evaluator plan: `clone-web/team-log/test_plan.md`
- Final contract: `clone-web/team-log/contract.md`
- Each eval round: `clone-web/team-log/eval_round_N.md`

### Acceptance criteria (Builder MUST hit, Evaluator MUST verify)
1. Selector renders at viewport 1440×900 (and 1920×1080 to verify centering) with pixel parity vs autodoc.es on first paint (after settle).
2. All category tabs are present in the correct order (e.g. Passenger Cars, Trucks, Motorcycles, …) and the active tab is visually distinguished with the same color/state.
3. Tabs are keyboard-/click-switchable; switching tabs re-renders the active panel.
4. Each active panel exposes the exact dropdowns the original does: at minimum **Make / Model / Engine** for the Passenger Cars tab (or whichever the original shows). When a Make is selected, the Model dropdown is populated; when Model is selected, Engine dropdown is populated (stubs/placeholders for missing data are acceptable as long as the UX flow matches).
5. Search submit button label, icon, and styling match the original.
6. Colors, font-family, font-size, font-weight, letter-spacing, line-height, border-radius, paddings, gaps for every selector element are extracted from the live site (via `javascript_tool` + `getComputedStyle`) — NOT eyeballed.
7. Dropdown panels open on click and close on outside-click / ESC, matching the original behavior. The open dropdown's `box-shadow`, `border-radius`, and option hover state must match.
8. Header/nav above the selector is present with logo + top utility links/cart at correct positions.
9. Background media under the selector (image/video/gradient) is reproduced; if a heavy WebGL/video asset exists, capture as a self-hosted `.mp4`/`.webm` or screenshot fallback.
10. Hover states on tabs, dropdowns, options, and CTA buttons implemented and observable in the screenshot.
11. Decorative separator / accent elements (orange or other brand accents) match the original tokens.
12. Selection within one tab persists visually (e.g. the chosen Make/Model shows in the chip area).
13. Responsive: doesn't break at 1280px and 1440px desktop width (mobile is OUT of scope unless trivially required).
14. Console clean, fonts load without FOUT, no external requests leak to autodoc.es.
15. The component is **isolatable** — opening `./autodoc-clone/` and the dev/preview URL must show ONLY the above-the-fold header + selector (not the full landing page sections).

### Validation commands
- `cd ./autodoc-clone && npm install && npm run build` → must exit 0
- `cd ./autodoc-clone && npm run dev` → must serve on a port; evaluator opens it and the live site at `https://www.autodoc.es/` and compares screenshots.
- No new files outside `./autodoc-clone/` and `clone-web/team-log/`.

### Rules
- Two browser-use workers (minimax-MiniMax-M3 each), separate sessions, adversarial roles.
- Builder does NOT self-evaluate. After compile/render success it reports files; Evaluator runs `eval_round_1.md`.
- Use `javascript_tool` for surgical CSS extraction (follow sniper script patterns in `clone_landing_page_101.md`).
- Trust the live site as source of truth. If tokens disagree with eyeballed estimates, the live DOM wins.

### Status
- planning → (after builder + evaluator plans merged) → in_progress → done

<END_OF_CONTENT>

kwargs.text:
## Startup gate — FIRST REPLY MUST CONFIRM
(1) You are in `browser-use` agent mode.
(2) Your model is `minimax-MiniMax-M3`.
Reply with those two confirmations before anything else.

## Role
You are the BUILDER for an autodoc.es above-the-fold vehicle-search-selector clone. You SEE and WRITE code. You do NOT self-evaluate.

## User Goal (verbatim — every fidelity word is a contract)
> Clone the main above-the-fold header section of https://www.autodoc.es/, specifically isolating the complete interactive vehicle search selector component (the multi-tab interface for selecting Passenger Cars, Trucks, Motorcycles, etc., by make, model, and engine). Save the full component inside './autodoc-clone'. It must be functionally and visually identical down to the pixel level, replicating all layout structures, dynamic dropdown behaviors, fonts, exact color tokens, hover states, animations, and any embedded media or layout effects.

## ⚠️ CRITICAL — Cloudflare blocker (prior run failed here)
autodoc.es is behind Cloudflare bot protection. A prior attempt using `fetch_url`/curl only captured the 'Just a moment...' challenge page (those broken refs are in `/app/clone-web/team-log/refs/`).
→ You MUST navigate using the REAL Chromium browser (`navigate` / `computer` browser tools). Do NOT use `fetch_url`, `bash curl`, or `wget` on autodoc.es — they return the challenge page.
→ After navigating, the browser must WAIT (8–15s) for the Cloudflare challenge to auto-solve. If an interactive 'Verify you are human' checkbox appears, CLICK it. Re-check that the URL bar shows the real autodoc.es content (Spanish vehicle selector visible) before extracting anything. If you still see 'Just a moment...', wait longer or retry navigation.
→ Save a clean snapshot of the real above-the-fold HTML to `/app/clone-web/team-log/refs/autodoc_live.html` ONLY after the challenge has cleared (verify it contains Spanish selector text like 'coches', 'marca', 'modelo', 'motor', NOT 'Just a moment').

## Scope
- IN: site header/nav (logo, top utility links, cart) + the interactive vehicle-search selector hero (tabs + make/model/engine dropdowns + search CTA + decorative background/media).
- OUT: everything below the fold (promo strip, category tiles, footer, newsletter). Do NOT clone.

## Workspace & references
- Work dir: `/app`. Read BOTH docs first: `/app/clone-web/clone_guide_v2.md` (follow implicitly) and `/app/clone-web/clone_landing_page_101.md` (deep reference, consult if stuck — esp. §5 sniper CSS scripts).
- Existing artifacts: `/app/clone-web/team-log/test_plan.md` (evaluator's plan — read it so your build matches its acceptance criteria) and `/root/.adal/engineer/contracts/2026-07-contract.md` (the full contract with 15 acceptance criteria).
- Output project: `/app/autodoc-clone/` (Vite + React + Tailwind).
- Builder plan: `/app/clone-web/team-log/builder_plan.md`.

## Process (per clone_guide_v2 phases)

### Phase 1 — Visual Discovery (DO THIS NOW)
1. Navigate to https://www.autodoc.es/ at 1440×900, wait 10–12s for Cloudflare + animations to settle, screenshot the above-the-fold. Save to `/app/clone-web/team-log/screens/orig_1440_settled.png`.
2. Resize to 1920×1080, wait 3s, screenshot → `orig_1920_settled.png`.
3. Resize to 390×844 (mobile reference only — to understand layout, NOT a build target), wait, screenshot.
4. Run the full animation/tech scanner (clone_guide_v2 §2.B) to detect canvas/video/iframes/gsap/lottie under the hero.
5. Run Sniper CSS extraction (`javascript_tool` IIFEs that JSON.stringify) on EVERY selector element: tabs (label, order, active state), dropdown fields per tab, CTA, header/logo, decorative background. Capture exact hex colors, fontFamily (strip 'Placeholder'), fontSize, fontWeight, lineHeight, letterSpacing, padding, gap, borderRadius, boxShadow, borders.
6. Capture fonts: enumerate `@font-face` rules + `performance.getEntriesByType('resource')` for `.woff2` URLs. Download/self-host.
7. Capture assets: logo SVG (inspect header only, not first svg), background image/video, data-URI backgrounds. If a video/canvas bg exists, capture as self-hosted `.mp4`/`.webm` or screenshot fallback.
8. Save the clean live HTML snapshot as described above once Cloudflare clears.

### Phase 2 — Write the Plan (DO NOT CODE YET)
Write `/app/clone-web/team-log/builder_plan.md` containing:
- Tab list + exact order + active-state tokens.
- Dropdown fields per tab (Make/Model/Engine + any others).
- Exact color/typography/spacing token table (hex + px values from live DOM).
- Assets to extract (logo, fonts, bg media) with source URLs.
- Animation/hover strategy.
- File structure for `autodoc-clone/`.
- Build validation: `npm install && npm run build` must exit 0; `npm run dev` serves selector.
- Open questions/risks (esp. Cloudflare, data for cascading dropdowns).
- A compact token sheet you'll later save to `/app/clone-web/team-log/tokens.md`.

After writing the plan, STOP and report. Wait for supervisor approval before coding. Reply with: startup gate confirmation, Cloudflare status (cleared? screenshot evidence?), discovery summary (tabs, key tokens, assets found), and the path to `builder_plan.md`.

## Rules
- Surgical scope: only the above-the-fold header + selector.
- Trust the live DOM over memory.
- Do NOT write any eval report.
- Keep Spanish labels verbatim (translating breaks pixel parity).
- Do NOT start coding until the supervisor approves your plan.
- If Cloudflare blocks you after 3 honest browser attempts, STOP and report exactly what you see — do NOT fabricate tokens from memory.

Save the full component inside './autodoc-clone'. ## ⚠️ CRITICAL — Cloudflare blocker (prior run failed here)
autodoc.es is behind Cloudflare bot protection. A prior attempt using `fetch_url`/curl only captured the 'Just a moment...' challenge page (those broken refs are in `/app/clone-web/team-log/refs/`).

Re-check that the URL bar shows the real autodoc.es content (Spanish vehicle selector visible) before extracting anything. If you still see 'Just a moment...', wait longer or retry navigation.
→ Save a clean snapshot of the real above-the-fold HTML to `/app/clone-web/team-log/refs/autodoc_live.html` ONLY after the challenge has cleared (verify it contains Spanish selector text like 'coches', 'marca', 'modelo', 'motor', NOT 'Just a moment').

## Scope
- IN: site header/nav (logo, top utility links, cart) + the interactive vehicle-search selector hero (tabs + make/model/engine dropdowns + search CTA + decorative background/media).
- OUT: everything below the fold (promo strip, category tiles, footer, newsletter). Do NOT clone.

## Workspace & references
- Work dir: `/app`. Read BOTH docs first: `/app/clone-web/clone_guide_v2.md` (follow implicitly) and `/app/clone-web/clone_landing_page_101.md` (deep reference, consult if stuck — esp. §5 sniper CSS scripts).

### Phase 1 — Visual Discovery (DO THIS NOW)

3. Resize to 390×844 (mobile reference only — to understand layout, NOT a build target), wait, screenshot.
4. Run the full animation/tech scanner (clone_guide_v2 §2.B) to detect canvas/video/iframes/gsap/lottie under the hero.
5. Run Sniper CSS extraction (`javascript_tool` IIFEs that JSON.stringify) on EVERY selector element: tabs (label, order, active state), dropdown fields per tab, CTA, header/logo, decorative background. Capture exact hex colors, fontFamily (strip 'Placeholder'), fontSize, fontWeight, lineHeight, letterSpacing, padding, gap, borderRadius, boxShadow, borders.
6. Capture fonts: enumerate `@font-face` rules + `performance.getEntriesByType('resource')` for `.woff2` URLs. Download/self-host.
7. Capture assets: logo SVG (inspect header only, not first svg), background image/video, data-URI backgrounds. If a video/canvas bg exists, capture as self-hosted `.mp4`/`.webm` or screenshot fallback.

I have summarized the information about AdaL's **Browser Use** mode based on the documentation:

### **How it Works & Backend**
*   **Backend:** AdaL's Browser Use mode uses **Playwright** as its underlying browser automation engine.
*   **Architecture:** It runs a **headless (or headed) browser instance** managed by the agent. While AdaL supports MCP-based browser tools (like the Playwright MCP server), the native `--agent-mode browser-use` integrates these capabilities directly into the agent's runtime for smoother multi-turn navigation.

### **Installation**
*   **Separate Install:** Yes, because AdaL uses Playwright, you must ensure the browser binaries are installed on your system.
*   **Command:** If it is your first time using it or if browsers are missing, you typically need to run:
    ```bash
    playwright install
    ```
    *(Note: If you installed AdaL via a system-level installer, this may be handled for you, but CLI users on Linux/WSL often need to run this manually.)*

### **Exposed Tools**
In `browser-use` mode, the agent has access to a specialized set of tools including:
*   `navigate`: Open a specific URL.
*   `click`: Interact with elements on the page.
*   `type`: Enter text into input fields.
*   `scroll`: Move up/down the page.
*   `screenshot`: Capture the current view (used for visual grounding).
*   `javascript_tool`: Execute custom JS in the page context.
*   `resize_window`: Change the viewport dimensions.

### **Troubleshooting: "No browser tools available"**
If the agent reports that no browser tools are available, it usually indicates one of the following:
1.  **Missing Dependencies:** Playwright is installed but the actual browser binaries (Chromium/Firefox) are missing. Run `playwright install`.
2.  **Incorrect Mode:** Ensure you started the session with `--agent-mode browser-use` or switched to it mid-session using the `/agent` command or by pressing **Tab**.
3.  **Environment Issues:** On Linux/WSL, you may need to install system dependencies for the browser:
    ```bash
    playwright install-deps
    ```

More: [https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog) (See entries for 1.1.1 and 1.2.3)

echo "=== chromium/playwright in PATH ===" && which chromium chromium-browser google-chrome chrome playwright 2>&1
echo "=== node_modules browser pkgs ===" && ls /app/node_modules 2>/dev/null | grep -iE "playwright|puppeteer|chrome|chromium" || echo "none in /app/node_modules"

echo "=== /root/.cache ===" && ls -la /root/.cache 2>/dev/null
echo "=== pip playwright ===" && pip show playwright 2>/dev/null | head -3 || echo "no pip playwright"
echo "=== find chrome/chromium binaries ===" && find / -maxdepth 6 -type f \( -iname "chrome" -o -iname "chromium" -o -iname "headless_shell" \) 2>/dev/null | head
echo "=== adal install ===" && which adal adal-dev 2>&1 && adal --version 2>&1 | head -2
echo "=== env browser vars ===" && env | grep -iE "browser|playwright|chrome|mcp|BROWSER_USE|CHROME" || echo "no browser env vars"

Content:
Title: Changelog | AdaL

URL Source: https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog

Markdown Content:
All notable changes to AdaL CLI will be documented in this file.

## [1.4.5] - 2026-07-09[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#145---2026-07-09 "Direct link to [1.4.5] - 2026-07-09")

*   Bug fix: Engineer mode workers now receive tasks instantly and reliably
*   xAI Grok 4.5 and Meta Muse Spark 1.1 now available via `/model`
*   Muse Spark 1.1 reasoning steps now stream live in the conversation

## [1.4.4] - 2026-07-09[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#144---2026-07-09 "Direct link to [1.4.4] - 2026-07-09")

*   xAI Grok 4.5 and Meta Muse Spark 1.1 now available via `/model`

## [1.4.3] - 2026-07-08[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#143---2026-07-08 "Direct link to [1.4.3] - 2026-07-08")

*   More reliable agent responses on longer conversations

## [1.4.1] - 2026-07-04[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#141---2026-07-04 "Direct link to [1.4.1] - 2026-07-04")

*   Smarter engineer mode — delegate tasks to supervised parallel workers with `--mode engineer`. The agent now adapts its process depth to match task complexity, skipping unnecessary steps on simple tasks and adding rigor on complex ones
*   More reliable conversations when using ZAI models with image-heavy history

## [1.4.0] - 2026-07-04[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#140---2026-07-04 "Direct link to [1.4.0] - 2026-07-04")

*   Engineer mode — delegate tasks to supervised parallel workers with `--mode engineer`
*   Multi-model consulting — the agent can now query multiple AI models in parallel and synthesize their answers for better results
*   More reliable installation and first-run experience across all platforms
*   Smoother onboarding flow

## [1.3.0] - 2026-07-01[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#130---2026-07-01 "Direct link to [1.3.0] - 2026-07-01")

*   AdaL Agent SDK — Python SDK for embedding AdaL's agent runtime in your applications. [https://github.com/SylphAI-Inc/adal-sdk](https://github.com/SylphAI-Inc/adal-sdk)
*   Custom system prompt - Override system role prompt for your customized use case [https://docs.sylph.ai/features/custom-system-prompt](https://docs.sylph.ai/features/custom-system-prompt)
*   Custom tools — define Python tools in `<project>/.adal/tools.py` and AdaL picks them up automatically [https://docs.sylph.ai/features/custom-tools](https://docs.sylph.ai/features/custom-tools)
*   Claude Sonnet 5 now available via `/model` — Anthropic's latest flagship with 1M context and extended thinking
*   New AdaL Pink theme (dark + light) — brand-colored theme available in `/theme`

## [1.2.4] - 2026-06-12[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#124---2026-06-12 "Direct link to [1.2.4] - 2026-06-12")

*   Claude Fable 5 now available via `/model` — Anthropic's new flagship with extended thinking
*   Desktop Home is workspace-first — pick a folder and press Enter to jump straight into a session
*   Improved light theme readability for code blocks and diffs across CLI and Web
*   File path actions in rendered markdown — click to open the source file or jump to Review
*   Side-by-side diff viewer now shows `+`/`−` markers for clearer change tracking
*   Desktop Review tab resumes reliably after reconnect — no more stale fallback states
*   File operations (delete, rename, move) in the Desktop file tree work correctly for all path formats
*   More accurate file-count badge on the current PR section

## [1.2.3] - 2026-06-03[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#123---2026-06-03 "Direct link to [1.2.3] - 2026-06-03")

*   Claude Opus 4.8 now available via `/model` — 1M context window with extended thinking
*   New `--thinking-effort` flag in headless mode (`adal -q`) — control reasoning depth per invocation from scripts and CI
*   Dotfiles (`.env`, `.adal`, `.vscode`) now visible in the Desktop and Web file tree sidebar
*   `/agent` is now the single command for all agent configuration — cleaner, one entry point
*   Unified `/compact` behavior across CLI, Desktop, and Web
*   Desktop sessions stay alive through screen sleep, lid close, and window minimize
*   Smarter browser automation — recovers automatically from stuck page sessions instead of giving up

## [1.2.2] - 2026-05-28[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#122---2026-05-28 "Direct link to [1.2.2] - 2026-05-28")

*   New `/agent` command — switch between Coding, Deep Research, and Browser Use modes from a single dialog
*   Shift+Tab now cycles through all permission modes: Strict → Accept Edits → YOLO
*   Tab key now toggles Plan mode only when in Coding mode
*   More reliable agent responses — eliminated a class of premature turn stops
*   Cleaner command cancellation — child processes no longer linger after Ctrl+C
*   Desktop: Full image support — paste, drag-and-drop, or send image-only queries
*   More reliable `/resume` — sessions restore cleanly without stale state errors
*   Removed deprecated Gemini 3 Pro from the model list

## [1.2.1] - 2026-05-23[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#121---2026-05-23 "Direct link to [1.2.1] - 2026-05-23")

*   Gemini 3.5 Flash model now available via `/model`
*   CLI: Context breakdown details now visible to all users in `/stats` session statistics
*   Desktop: Marked desktop as preview in header
*   Desktop: Seamless `/ide` handoff — Desktop app stays connected without backend interruption
*   Desktop: Clickable file paths in chat messages — click any inline path to open it in the file viewer
*   Desktop: Improved file viewer with better media previews and smart link navigation
*   Desktop: Smoother Desktop updates — progress shown in-app instead of native modal dialogs
*   Desktop: More reliable sessions after auto-updates (no more stale certificate errors)

## [1.2.0] - 2026-05-20[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#120---2026-05-20 "Direct link to [1.2.0] - 2026-05-20")

*   (Preview) [Desktop App](https://docs.sylph.ai/getting-started/desktop-app) now available - download app and open with icon or `/ide` command
*   (Preview) Self-Review Agent available in Desktop App - open your PR and the agent auto-clusters and explains huge reviews for you
*   Smarter multi-turn conversations with extended thinking models
*   More reliable command execution with clearer error reporting when shell commands fail
*   Images you paste or upload persist correctly when resuming or switching sessions

## [1.1.4] - 2026-05-15[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#114---2026-05-15 "Direct link to [1.1.4] - 2026-05-15")

*   (Preview) `/ide` command now available - open your session in the browser with one keystroke
*   (Preview) self-review agent feature now available in `/ide` - open your PR and the agent auto-clusters and explains huge reviews for you
*   More reliable auto-updates on Windows
*   Improved instruction following and tool accuracy across all models
*   Improved cross-platform reliability on Windows with non-UTF-8 locales

## [1.1.3] - 2026-05-09[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#113---2026-05-09 "Direct link to [1.1.3] - 2026-05-09")

*   Unified tool expand/collapse toggle for cleaner message display
*   Faster first-query response times
*   Improved token tracking accuracy for all Chat Completions providers
*   Subagents run more reliably across providers

## [1.1.1] - 2026-05-05[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#111---2026-05-05 "Direct link to [1.1.1] - 2026-05-05")

*   Improved subagent performance and token efficiency
*   Fixed Anthropic multiple image input inconsistencies
*   Added support for reading SVG files
*   Added a native installer for Windows users
*   Added Browser Use mode; press Tab to toggle among Plan, Deep Research, and Browser Use modes

## [1.0.9] - 2026-05-01[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#109---2026-05-01 "Direct link to [1.0.9] - 2026-05-01")

*   Improved the UX of image gen with GPT and Gemini models
*   Fixed the windows crash bugs reported and improved product reliability

## [1.0.8] - 2026-04-25[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#108---2026-04-25 "Direct link to [1.0.8] - 2026-04-25")

*   Fixed image generation error and improved product reliability

## [1.0.7] - 2026-04-25[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#107---2026-04-25 "Direct link to [1.0.7] - 2026-04-25")

*   Added GPT-5.5 model support
*   First week 50%-off promotion for new models include GPT, DeepSeek, KIMI and Claude

## [1.0.6] - 2026-04-24[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#106---2026-04-24 "Direct link to [1.0.6] - 2026-04-24")

*   Fixed known issues in new model clients(DeepSeek & KIMI)

## [1.0.5] - 2026-04-24[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#105---2026-04-24 "Direct link to [1.0.5] - 2026-04-24")

*   Added GPT Image 2 support for image generation
*   Added DeepSeek V4 Flash&Pro models
*   Added KIMI 2.6 model
*   Minor UX polish and bug fixes

## [1.0.4] - 2026-04-20[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#104---2026-04-20 "Direct link to [1.0.4] - 2026-04-20")

*   Added support for Claude Opus 4.7 models
*   Minor UX polish and bug fixes

## [1.0.3] - 2026-04-16[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#103---2026-04-16 "Direct link to [1.0.3] - 2026-04-16")

*   Added Cron Agent support and interactive `/cron` management in the CLI
*   Fixed ChatGPT subscription streaming issues
*   Fixed Anthropic model image input handling
*   Improved error message handling

## [1.0.2] - 2026-04-03[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#102---2026-04-03 "Direct link to [1.0.2] - 2026-04-03")

*   Improved error messages display
*   Improved image generation requests stability

## [1.0.1] - 2026-03-31[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#101---2026-03-31 "Direct link to [1.0.1] - 2026-03-31")

*   Optimized the running memory of the AdaL session process
*   Improved Claude model requests stability
*   Added worktree info in exit UX if applicable

## [1.0.0] - 2026-03-27[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#100---2026-03-27 "Direct link to [1.0.0] - 2026-03-27")

*   Introduced Deep Research, a dedicated mode for investigating complex tasks and delivering structured reports (Tab to toggle)
*   Added Linux ARM64 support
*   Fixed Linux lagging issue
*   Enabled long-running agent execution when YOLO mode is toggled via /permissions
*   Improved streaming error handling and auto-retry
*   Added terminal notifications when AdaL needs user attention
*   Improved MCP user experience with smoother authentication and stable connections
*   Added a `--resume` flag for resuming previous sessions by ID
*   Resolved an issue affecting skill installation
*   Fixed slash-prefixed messages can't be queued mid-turn
*   Fetch tool display enrichment
*   Fixed an issue on WSL where pressing i for more info could fail silently
*   Improved chat history display with progressive loading
*   Fixed resumed-session display issues for subagents
*   Simplified compression report display
*   AdaL Web: Improved feature parity with AdaL CLI
*   AdaL Web: a redesigned UI
*   AdaL Web: added file view support
*   AdaL Web: Fixed browser not auto-opening on WSL

## [0.9.9] - 2026-03-18[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#099---2026-03-18 "Direct link to [0.9.9] - 2026-03-18")

*   Added MiniMax M2.7 and M2.7 Highspeed models
*   Added YOLO mode to auto-approve all tools and bash commands (/permissions)

## [0.9.8] - 2026-03-17[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#098---2026-03-17 "Direct link to [0.9.8] - 2026-03-17")

*   Improved Claude streaming reliability with fewer timeout errors
*   Improved error messages for rate limits for clarity

## [0.9.7] - 2026-03-16[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#097---2026-03-16 "Direct link to [0.9.7] - 2026-03-16")

*   Added GLM-5-Turbo from Z.ai
*   Reduced TimeoutError for Claude Opus models to allow long responses to pass

## [0.9.6] - 2026-03-14[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#096---2026-03-14 "Direct link to [0.9.6] - 2026-03-14")

*   Fixed MCP servers incorrectly showing as "not ready" after successful authentication

## [0.9.5] - 2026-03-11[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#095---2026-03-11 "Direct link to [0.9.5] - 2026-03-11")

*   Improved reliability for GLM requests

## [0.9.4] - 2026-03-11[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#094---2026-03-11 "Direct link to [0.9.4] - 2026-03-11")

*   Improved image generation reliability
*   Fixed incorrect mapping of queued @file references to images
*   Corrected hidden-line counts in diff view
*   Resolved a plugin install issue affecting some marketplace sources
*   Consolidated `adal --help` information
*   Renamed `adal workspace` to `adal worktree` and simplified its usage
*   Added `--model` support for headless mode to specify models
*   Added a clear "Text-only" capability indicator in model selection for non-vision models
*   Minor UI improvements for assistant messages

## [0.9.3] - 2026-03-07[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#093---2026-03-07 "Direct link to [0.9.3] - 2026-03-07")

*   Terminal tab title now shows AdaL
*   Bug fix in edit file path resolution

## [0.9.2] - 2026-03-06[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#092---2026-03-06 "Direct link to [0.9.2] - 2026-03-06")

*   Improved subagent performance
*   Added automatic subagent folding after completion
*   Further optimized prompt caching
*   Added @ autocomplete directory suggestions
*   Refined /model panel UI

## [0.9.1] - 2026-03-05[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#091---2026-03-05 "Direct link to [0.9.1] - 2026-03-05")

*   Added GPT-5.4 in ChatGPT subscription
*   Optimized cache hit rate
*   Optimized error self-recovery and subagent performance
*   AdaL Web: aligned stream/subagent behavior with AdaL CLI

## [0.9.0] - 2026-03-05[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#090---2026-03-05 "Direct link to [0.9.0] - 2026-03-05")

*   Added subagent support to improve AdaL's efficiency and performance
*   Optimized prompt caching to make AdaL 2x cost-effective
*   Added GPT-5.3 Codex and GPT-5.4 from OpenAI and set GPT-5.3 Codex as the current default model
*   Added GLM-5, GLM-4.7 FlashX and 3 more models from Zai
*   Upgraded image generation to Nano Banana 2 and enabled image editing
*   Memory optimization improvements for smoother long sessions
*   Corrected the effective input context window for all OpenAI models from 400k to 272k
*   Bug fixes in Ollama local model support
*   Refined /model panel metadata, pricing display, and selector UX
*   Removed /auth and merged auth details into /about
*   Keyboard shortcuts now respond to both lowercase and uppercase keys
*   Slash autocomplete completes commands without auto-running, allowing arguments to be added before execution
*   Fixed and optimized scrollbar behavior
*   Added context percentage display in the footer
*   Fixed inaccurate line counts in “click to expand”

## [0.8.5] - 2026-02-28[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#085---2026-02-28 "Direct link to [0.8.5] - 2026-02-28")

*   Fixed GPT models via ChatGPT subscription returning "Unsupported parameter" errors

## [0.8.4] - 2026-02-27[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#084---2026-02-27 "Direct link to [0.8.4] - 2026-02-27")

*   Fixed GPT models crashing during long sessions
*   Improved context window utilization to enable longer conversations before compaction
*   Deprecated older OpenAI models (GPT-4.1, o4-mini, standard GPT-5/5.1) and prioritized Codex variants
*   Added GPT-5.2 Codex as a recommended model

## [0.8.3] - 2026-02-25[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#083---2026-02-25 "Direct link to [0.8.3] - 2026-02-25")

*   Added image generation tool via Google Gemini's Nano Banana
*   Supported using ChatGPT subscription in adal to use OpenAI models
*   Added headless mode to run adal in non-interactive CLI execution (`adal -q "query"`)
*   Supported select text to copy on all major platforms and terminals
*   Supported copy images from screenshots
*   Unified design on click to expand and collapse
*   Move status notification to display on the same row as the loading indicator for a more compact UI layout

## [0.8.2] - 2026-02-21[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#082---2026-02-21 "Direct link to [0.8.2] - 2026-02-21")

*   Faster MCP startup
*   Sync MCP tools when switching models

## [0.8.1] - 2026-02-21[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#081---2026-02-21 "Direct link to [0.8.1] - 2026-02-21")

*   Added read image tool to analyze images directly
*   Improved file editing with fewer retries and more reliable edits
*   Local model support via ollama (preview)

## [0.8.0] - 2026-02-19[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#080---2026-02-19 "Direct link to [0.8.0] - 2026-02-19")

*   Improved agentic tool use and communication across all models
*   New models - Google: Gemini 3.1 Pro
*   New models - MiniMax: M2.5 and M2.5 Highspeed
*   New models - Anthropic: Claude Sonnet 4.6
*   New models — OpenAI: GPT-5.2 Codex and 9 more
*   Redesigned /model dialog with provider-specific sections
*   Supported click to expand and collapse for lengthy display
*   Improved diff view with inline change highlighting
*   Overlay-style floating header on the top right corner
*   New theme selection with configurable terminal background
*   New user query UI design
*   Improved bash confirmation dialog layout
*   AdaL Web: real-time git branch updates and auto-refresh file explorer on branch change
*   AdaL Web: diff viewer in the sidebar for reviewing changes

## [0.7.1] - 2026-02-12[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#071---2026-02-12 "Direct link to [0.7.1] - 2026-02-12")

*   Improved @ search by skipping autocomplete when a space follows @
*   Fixed file editing issues on Windows
*   Reduced hallucination and improved error self-recovery
*   Bash confirmation dialog now shows agent description for better context
*   AdaL Web: smart scrolling with automatic scroll-to-bottom on query submission
*   AdaL Web: added AGENTS.md creation in the sidebar

## [0.7.0] - 2026-02-10[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#070---2026-02-10 "Direct link to [0.7.0] - 2026-02-10")

*   AdaL Web (Preview): a new browser-based interface for AdaL that can access core capabilities (`adal --web`)
*   Blazing-fast file search performance
*   Smarter auto-retry for model provider issues
*   Recommended models section in /model for quick access to top picks

## [0.6.3] - 2026-02-05[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#063---2026-02-05 "Direct link to [0.6.3] - 2026-02-05")

*   Added Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context window)
*   Always-on adaptive thinking that removes the thinking toggle
*   Loading indicator UI improvements
*   Model tags and display name cleanup

## [0.6.2] - 2026-02-05[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#062---2026-02-05 "Direct link to [0.6.2] - 2026-02-05")

*   Added Claude Opus 4.6 day 1 support (/model)
*   Improved error handling and retry experience
*   Smoother tool cancellation experience
*   Improved workspace management to enable parallel development
*   Optimized UI display for input area and toggle displays

## [0.6.1] - 2026-02-02[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#061---2026-02-02 "Direct link to [0.6.1] - 2026-02-02")

*   Simplified thinking mode from 3 modes to 2 modes (on/off)
*   Better scrolling experience with automatic scroll-to-bottom on query submission
*   Automatic retry logic for network errors
*   Better error handling and recovery
*   Minor bug fixes

## [0.6.0] - 2026-01-31[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#060---2026-01-31 "Direct link to [0.6.0] - 2026-01-31")

*   Parallel tool calls for ~50% faster execution and lower token cost
*   Lightning-fast memory compaction averaging 8s with more effective context retention
*   Better error handling with faster failure on context overflow
*   More accurate @ reference search navigation
*   Improved agent memory management
*   Improved session resume with consistent thinking content and message counts
*   Skills auto-update and improved UI
*   Supported using skills CLI to install skills to AdaL
*   Changed default model to Claude Opus 4.5
*   Clearer /init onboarding experience
*   Added 'i' keyboard shortcut in dialogs to open documentation
*   Supported Mermaid diagram rendering
*   Fixed table border misalignment and code block highlighting in answer rendering
*   Added current model name below the input box

## [0.5.4] - 2026-01-28[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#054---2026-01-28 "Direct link to [0.5.4] - 2026-01-28")

*   Minor bug fixes for compaction and session resume

## [0.5.3] - 2026-01-27[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#053---2026-01-27 "Direct link to [0.5.3] - 2026-01-27")

*   Faster @ reference search navigation
*   Workspace management to enable parallel development
*   Visual separator marker showing where compaction occurred when resuming sessions
*   Ctrl+R (thought toggle) and Ctrl+C (cancel/exit) now work when dialogs are open
*   Show changelog after auto update

## [0.5.2] - 2026-01-24[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#052---2026-01-24 "Direct link to [0.5.2] - 2026-01-24")

*   Thinking mode on by default
*   Better view when resuming sessions
*   Removed redundant aliases for a tidier command palette
*   Added spinner for bash tool execution display
*   Clearer plan mode separators in message history
*   Unified notification toasts for copy actions
*   Theme improvements
*   Other minor bug fixes

## [0.5.1] - 2026-01-15[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#051---2026-01-15 "Direct link to [0.5.1] - 2026-01-15")

*   Improved scrolling experience across all platforms
*   Smarter code display with automatic line wrapping in diffs and code blocks
*   Fixed known bugs in /model and /theme selections
*   Consolidated keyboard shortcuts and slash commands
*   Use Ctrl+C to cancel streaming anytime
*   Git-aware file operations that preserve history, better multi-line commit messages

## [0.5.0] - 2026-01-13[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#050---2026-01-13 "Direct link to [0.5.0] - 2026-01-13")

*   New UI: more stable, no flashing, no flickering, faster performance throughout the session
*   Improved input experience: cursor navigation, Shift+Enter for multi-line input, better query history navigation via up/down arrow
*   New logo and header design
*   Simplified /theme
*   Better markdown formatting and table rendering
*   Enhanced image understanding
*   Improved /compact
*   Enhanced /changelog with more history and link to documentation
*   Fixed auto-edit UI not updating after Shift+Tab or option selection during edit confirmations

## [0.4.0] - 2026-01-07[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#040---2026-01-07 "Direct link to [0.4.0] - 2026-01-07")

*   Supported more Linux distributions
*   Added Plan Mode (Ctrl+P) for planning-first workflows
*   Added google-gemini-3-flash-preview (/model)
*   Removed google-gemini-flash-2.5 and gpt-4o

## [0.3.5] - 2026-01-05[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#035---2026-01-05 "Direct link to [0.3.5] - 2026-01-05")

*   Faster query response
*   Supported more general web content for websearch, including images, places, and more
*   Fixed issues of fetching URL content
*   Fixed the "unknown slash command" issues
*   Improved bug reporting (/bug)
*   Enhanced file path handling

## [0.3.4] - 2026-01-01[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#034---2026-01-01 "Direct link to [0.3.4] - 2026-01-01")

*   Full support for skills (/skills): plugins/marketplace, personal and project skills
*   Improved branch display with cleaner format showing directory and branch
*   More robust auto-compact
*   Faster and more token efficient manual compact (/compact)
*   Added GPT-4o model support with robust output parsing
*   Enhanced error recovery
*   Other minor bug fixes and improvements

## [0.3.3] - 2025-12-24[​](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog#033---2025-12-24 "Direct link to [0.3.3] - 2025-12-24")

I have summarized the information about AdaL's **Browser Use** mode based on the documentation:

### **How it Works & Backend**
*   **Backend:** AdaL's Browser Use mode uses **Playwright** as its underlying browser automation engine.
*   **Architecture:** It runs a **headless (or headed) browser instance** managed by the agent. While AdaL supports MCP-based browser tools (like the Playwright MCP server), the native `--agent-mode browser-use` integrates these capabilities directly into the agent's runtime for smoother multi-turn navigation.

### **Installation**
*   **Separate Install:** Yes, because AdaL uses Playwright, you must ensure the browser binaries are installed on your system.
*   **Command:** If it is your first time using it or if browsers are missing, you typically need to run:
    ```bash
    playwright install
    ```
    *(Note: If you installed AdaL via a system-level installer, this may be handled for you, but CLI users on Linux/WSL often need to run this manually.)*

### **Exposed Tools**
In `browser-use` mode, the agent has access to a specialized set of tools including:
*   `navigate`: Open a specific URL.
*   `click`: Interact with elements on the page.
*   `type`: Enter text into input fields.
*   `scroll`: Move up/down the page.
*   `screenshot`: Capture the current view (used for visual grounding).
*   `javascript_tool`: Execute custom JS in the page context.
*   `resize_window`: Change the viewport dimensions.

### **Troubleshooting: "No browser tools available"**
If the agent reports that no browser tools are available, it usually indicates one of the following:
1.  **Missing Dependencies:** Playwright is installed but the actual browser binaries (Chromium/Firefox) are missing. Run `playwright install`.
2.  **Incorrect Mode:** Ensure you started the session with `--agent-mode browser-use` or switched to it mid-session using the `/agent` command or by pressing **Tab**.
3.  **Environment Issues:** On Linux/WSL, you may need to install system dependencies for the browser:
    ```bash
    playwright install-deps
    ```

More: [https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog](https://docs.sylph.ai/changelog) (See entries for 1.1.1 and 1.2.3)

=== adal --help for browser/install ===
Usage: adal [options]

AdaL - AI Agent for software engineering

  adal                  Start interactive mode (default)
  adal -q "query"       Run a single query and exit
  adal plugin <cmd>     Manage plugins & marketplaces
  adal worktree <cmd>   Manage AdaL worktrees
  adal --ide            Launch IDE (desktop app or browser)
  adal --ide -r <id>    Resume a session in IDE

Headless options:
  -q, --query <text>    Query to execute
  -r, --resume <id>     Resume a previous session by ID (use with -q)
  -m, --model <name>    Model registry key for this headless query
                          See full model list: https://docs.sylph.ai/getting-started/models
  -o, --output <format> Output format: text, json, or stream-json
  --enabled-default-tools <list>  Positive set — ONLY these tools are available (e.g., "Read,Search,Bash")
                          Groups: Bash, Edit, Read, Search, Web, Image, Video, Consult
  --disabled-default-tools <list> Disable core tools/groups entirely — invisible & unexecutable (e.g., "Web,Video")
  --thinking-effort <level>  Set reasoning effort for this run (e.g., low,
                          medium, high, max). Allowed values depend on the
                          active model. Persisted to model config.
  --agent-mode <mode>   Main agent mode at startup: coding (default),
                          browser-use, or research. Promotes the agent before
                          the query runs (headless equivalent of /agent).
  --yolo                Auto-approve all tool executions (no confirmation prompts).
                        You accept full responsibility for any changes made.
  --mode <mode>         Agent mode: code, video, research, or engineer

SDK options:
  --sdk-runtime         Start as persistent stdio NDJSON runtime for the
                        Python Agent SDK. Reads commands from stdin, streams
                        events to stdout. Not for interactive use.

General:
  -w, --ide             Launch AdaL Desktop IDE
  --web                 (deprecated, alias for --ide)
  -v, --version         Show version
  --help                Show this help

Plugin management:
  adal plugin marketplace add <src>   Add marketplace
  adal plugin install <ref>           Install plugin (name@marketplace)
  adal plugin list                    List installed plugins

Examples:
  adal -q "Fix the bug in main.py"
  adal -q "Summarize repo" -m openai-gpt-5.4 --output json
  adal -q "Continue the work" -r <session-id>
  adal plugin marketplace add SylphAI-Inc/skills
  adal plugin install core-skills@adal-agent-skills
  adal worktree create -b fix
  adal worktree remove fix

Usage: adal [options]

AdaL - AI Agent for software engineering

  adal worktree <cmd>   Manage AdaL worktrees
  adal --ide            Launch IDE (desktop app or browser)
  adal --ide -r <id>    Resume a session in IDE

Headless options:
  -q, --query <text>    Query to execute
  -r, --resume <id>     Resume a previous session by ID (use with -q)
  -m, --model <name>    Model registry key for this headless query
                          See full model list: https://docs.sylph.ai/getting-started/models
  -o, --output <format> Output format: text, json, or stream-json
  --enabled-default-tools <list>  Positive set — ONLY these tools are available (e.g., "Read,Search,Bash")
                          Groups: Bash, Edit, Read, Search, Web, Image, Video, Consult
  --disabled-default-tools <list> Disable core tools/groups entirely — invisible & unexecutable (e.g., "Web,Video")
  --thinking-effort <level>  Set reasoning effort for this run (e.g., low,
                          medium, high, max). Allowed values depend on the
                          active model. Persisted to model config.
  --agent-mode <mode>   Main agent mode at startup: coding (default),

SDK options:

General:
  -w, --ide             Launch AdaL Desktop IDE
  --web                 (deprecated, alias for --ide)
  -v, --version         Show version
  --help                Show this help

Plugin management:
  adal plugin marketplace add <src>   Add marketplace
  adal plugin install <ref>           Install plugin (name@marketplace)
  adal plugin list                    List installed plugins

Examples:

  adal -q "Continue the work" -r <session-id>
  adal plugin marketplace add SylphAI-Inc/skills

  adal worktree create -b fix
  adal worktree remove fix
  adal --ide

=== adal --help for browser/install ===
Usage: adal [options]

AdaL - AI Agent for software engineering

  adal                  Start interactive mode (default)
  adal -q "query"       Run a single query and exit
  adal plugin <cmd>     Manage plugins & marketplaces
  adal worktree <cmd>   Manage AdaL worktrees
  adal --ide            Launch IDE (desktop app or browser)
  adal --ide -r <id>    Resume a session in IDE

Headless options:
  -q, --query <text>    Query to execute
  -r, --resume <id>     Resume a previous session by ID (use with -q)
  -m, --model <name>    Model registry key for this headless query
                          See full model list: https://docs.sylph.ai/getting-started/models
  -o, --output <format> Output format: text, json, or stream-json
  --enabled-default-tools <list>  Positive set — ONLY these tools are available (e.g., "Read,Search,Bash")
                          Groups: Bash, Edit, Read, Search, Web, Image, Video, Consult
  --disabled-default-tools <list> Disable core tools/groups entirely — invisible & unexecutable (e.g., "Web,Video")
  --thinking-effort <level>  Set reasoning effort for this run (e.g., low,
                          medium, high, max). Allowed values depend on the
                          active model. Persisted to model config.
  --agent-mode <mode>   Main agent mode at startup: coding (default),
                          browser-use, or research. Promotes the agent before
                          the query runs (headless equivalent of /agent).
  --yolo                Auto-approve all tool executions (no confirmation prompts).
                        You accept full responsibility for any changes made.
  --mode <mode>         Agent mode: code, video, research, or engineer

SDK options:
  --sdk-runtime         Start as persistent stdio NDJSON runtime for the
                        Python Agent SDK. Reads commands from stdin, streams
                        events to stdout. Not for interactive use.

General:
  -w, --ide             Launch AdaL Desktop IDE
  --web                 (deprecated, alias for --ide)
  -v, --version         Show version
  --help                Show this help

Plugin management:
  adal plugin marketplace add <src>   Add marketplace
  adal plugin install <ref>           Install plugin (name@marketplace)
  adal plugin list                    List installed plugins

Examples:
  adal -q "Fix the bug in main.py"
  adal -q "Summarize repo" -m openai-gpt-5.4 --output json
  adal -q "Continue the work" -r <session-id>
  adal plugin marketplace add SylphAI-Inc/skills
  adal plugin install core-skills@adal-agent-skills
  adal worktree create -b fix
  adal worktree remove fix
  adal --ide

const fs = require('fs');
const path = require('path');
const src = path.join(__dirname, 'opentui-native', '@opentui');
const dest = path.join(__dirname, 'node_modules', '@opentui');
if (fs.existsSync(src)) {
  fs.mkdirSync(dest, { recursive: true });
  fs.readdirSync(src).forEach(pkg => {
    const srcPkg = path.join(src, pkg);
    const destPkg = path.join(dest, pkg);
    if (!fs.existsSync(destPkg)) {
      fs.cpSync(srcPkg, destPkg, { recursive: true });
    }
  });
}
=== adal launcher script ===
#!/usr/bin/env sh
# Resolve symlinks to find actual script location
SCRIPT_PATH="$0"
while [ -L "$SCRIPT_PATH" ]; do
  SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$SCRIPT_PATH")" && pwd)"
  SCRIPT_PATH="$(readlink "$SCRIPT_PATH")"
  [ "${SCRIPT_PATH#/}" = "$SCRIPT_PATH" ] && SCRIPT_PATH="$SCRIPT_DIR/$SCRIPT_PATH"
done
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$SCRIPT_PATH")" && pwd)"
exec "$SCRIPT_DIR/runtime/bun" "$SCRIPT_DIR/adal-cli.js" "$@"

The worker output shows the search isn't finding playwright/chromium binaries in the bundled backend. The CHANGELOG confirms Browser Use mode exists (line 115, 66) and mentions "recovers automatically from stuck page sessions." But the actual browser backend must be downloaded/installed separately.

Actually, this is getting deep. Let me step back and think strategically.

The core problem: browser-use mode requires a browser (Playwright/Chromium), and this container doesn't have one installed, and it's not bundled with adal-cli. To use browser-use I'd need to install Playwright + Chromium + system deps, which is a heavy operation requiring apt-get (system deps for Chromium) and downloading ~150MB browser binaries. And critically — I have NO python3 installed system-wide (the adal backend must bundle its own). And no curl/wget.

But also — even if I install Chromium, beating Cloudflare's turnstile from an automated headless browser is genuinely hard. Cloudflare detects headless Chromium. This may not even work.

Let me reconsider the whole approach. This is a high-stakes situation: the user wants a pixel-perfect clone of an interactive component from a site that's (a) behind Cloudflare bot protection and (b) the browser tooling isn't installed.

Let me think about what's realistic:

Option A: Install Playwright/Chromium, try to beat Cloudflare. Risk: heavy install, Cloudflare may still block headless, lots of time, may fail.

Option B: Find an alternative way to get the real HTML. Maybe a different autodoc regional domain isn't behind Cloudflare? Or the web archive (Wayback Machine)? Or Google cache? The user specifically said autodoc.es. Let me check if there's a cached/archived version of autodoc.es that I can fetch (fetch_url works for regular pages).

Actually — autodoc has many European domains (autodoc.de, autodoc.co.uk, autodoc.fr, autodoc.it, etc.). They likely share the same selector component template. Some might not be behind Cloudflare, or the Wayback Machine might have a snapshot. The component is what matters for pixel-parity.

But the user explicitly said autodoc.es and "functionally and visually identical down to the pixel level" — so I need the .es version specifically. However the Spanish labels would be the same across the autodoc template; the design tokens would be identical across regional sites (same template). For pixel parity of the COMPONENT, any autodoc regional site gives the same tokens. But to honor the user's intent, .es is the target.

Let me think about Wayback Machine. fetch_url can fetch web.archive.org snapshots. That could give me the real HTML/CSS/JS of autodoc.es as captured historically. That's a legitimate path that bypasses Cloudflare entirely. The component design is unlikely to have changed dramatically.

Actually, let me reconsider. Let me check the metadata jsonl for the prior 3e9753e3 session (27 messages, 3.5MB conversation) — that was a previous engineer attempt that may have already figured out the Cloudflare situation and maybe already obtained real tokens. Let me check the two viable install paths — apt chromium and npm puppeteer (which bundles its own Chromium) — and whether the AdaL backend even looks for Playwright. <tools_1fec>[{"name": "bash", "kwargs": {"command": <raw_1fec>echo "=== which strings ===" && which strings grep -a 2>&1
echo "=== grep -a backend for playwright/ms-playwright ===" 
grep -a -o -iE "ms-playwright|playwright|chromium-linux|/\.cache/ms-playwright" /usr/local/lib/node_modules/@sylphai/adal-cli/node_modules/@sylphai/adal-cli-linux-x64/backend/adal-backend/adal-backend 2>/dev/null | sort | uniq -c | head
echo "=== apt chromium availability ===" && apt-get install -y --dry-run chromium 2>&1 | tail -5 || echo "chromium not in apt directly"
echo "=== apt-cache search chromium ===" && apt-cache search chromium 2>/dev/null | head -10
echo "=== apt-cache search playwright ===" && apt-cache search playwright 2>/dev/null | head
echo "=== disk space ===" && df -h / 2>/dev/null | tail -2
echo "=== can npm install puppeteer? (dry registry check only) ===" && node -e "fetch('https://registry.npmjs.org/puppeteer').then(r=>console.log('puppeteer:',r.status)).catch(e=>console.log('ERR',e.message))" 2>&1
echo "=== npm install playwright reg check ===" && node -e "fetch('https://registry.npmjs.org/playwright').then(r=>console.log('playwright:',r.status)).catch(e=>console.log('ERR',e.message))" 2>&1</raw_1fec>, "skip_approval": true}}]</tools_1fec>

metadata.thinking:
The environment situation:
- No `python3`, no `curl`, no `wget`, no `strings`
- No Chromium/Playwright installed anywhere
- apt-cache has no chromium/playwright packages (package lists may not include them — Debian 12 has `chromium` in non-free or it needs the snap, but apt search returned nothing, suggesting package lists are minimal/not updated)
- npm registry reachable (200) for both puppeteer and playwright
- AdaL backend binary is a self-contained 19MB binary (Python embedded via PyInstaller likely — `_internal` with dist-info folders). No Playwright strings found.
- autodoc.es returns 403 to plain fetch (Cloudflare)

The key question: does AdaL's `--agent-mode browser-use` use Playwright that I need to install separately, or does it have its own mechanism? The adal_support said it uses Playwright and I need to run `playwright install`. But there's no python3 on the system and no playwright package visible. The backend is a PyInstaller-frozen binary.

Wait — the strings/grep -a found NOTHING for playwright in the backend binary. That's suspicious. Let me reconsider. The grep -a returned nothing for "ms-playwright|playwright|chromium-linux". Maybe the binary is compressed/packed (PyInstaller archives are compressed).

Actually, the real issue: adal_support says browser-use needs Playwright + `playwright install`. The prior engineer session (3.5MB transcript, 143× 403, 40× cloudflare, 6× "no browser") clearly struggled and may NOT have solved it. The metadata shows that session had total_messages 27 but I should check if it produced any actual clone output. The autodoc-clone dir is empty. So the prior session did NOT succeed.

Let me think about the most pragmatic path. The task requires:
1. A real browser to beat Cloudflare and extract live tokens
2. Building a pixel-faithful clone

Options:
A. Install Playwright via npm in /app, get chromium, then... but how does the AdaL browser-use worker use it? The worker uses its own backend. If the backend is PyInstaller-frozen and expects `playwright` python package + browsers at `/root/.cache/ms-playwright`, installing the npm playwright won't help the python backend.

Actually — let me reconsider. Maybe AdaL browser-use mode uses a DIFFERENT mechanism. Let me check if there's an MCP browser tool, or if browser-use downloads chromium to ms-playwright on first use. The adal_support mentioned changelog entries 1.1.1 and 1.2.3.

Let me think about what's realistic here. The environment is quite stripped (no python, no curl). Installing Playwright Python into a frozen binary isn't possible. 

Alternative approach: I could install Playwright via npm and have the BUILDER worker use a headless chromium directly through node scripts to (a) bypass Cloudflare and (b) extract tokens. But the worker is a browser-use agent that expects `navigate`/`computer`/`javascript_tool` tools — if those don't exist, the worker can't do visual work.

Wait. Let me reconsider the whole thing. Maybe I should try actually running `adal --agent-mode browser-use` as a headless single query and see what happens — does it auto-download chromium? Let me check the changelog and maybe just try triggering browser mode to see the error.

Actually, the most efficient diagnostic: start the browser-use worker and have it attempt ONE navigate, and capture the actual error. The worker already told me "no browser tools available" — but maybe that's because the worker checked `which chromium` rather than actually trying its built-in tools. Let me re-examine: the worker said "My function set has no navigate, computer, javascript_tool, or resize_window." That's the worker's self-report. But workers don't always accurately know their own toolset. 

Hmm, but actually the worker ran `bash` steps and read files — it's behaving as a coding agent, not browser-use. Let me verify by checking the worker's actual available tools. Let me get_worker_output to see, or better, directly ask the worker to LIST its tools or attempt a navigate call.

Actually the cleaner path: let me check the AdaL docs/changelog for the exact browser-use setup, and simultaneously try a headless test. Let me consult adal_support more specifically AND check if there's a `playwright install` equivalent, AND look at whether browser-use needs a one-time `adal` setup command.

Let me be decisive. The pragmatic engineering move:
1. Install system deps + chromium via Playwright npm (puppeteer bundles chromium, or `npx playwright install chromium`). This gets a real Chromium on disk.
2. BUT the AdaL browser-use backend is frozen Python — it needs the Python `playwright` package + browsers in ms-playwright cache. The frozen `_internal` doesn't include playwright (grep found nothing, though compressed).

Let me actually verify whether the frozen backend includes playwright by checking _internal contents more carefully — grep found nothing but the archive may be compressed inside the binary. Let me check the _internal dir for a playwright folder.

Actually I already did `find ... -iname "*playwright*"` on the backend and found nothing. So the frozen backend does NOT bundle playwright.

This strongly suggests browser-use mode downloads/uses playwright at runtime, OR it's genuinely not set up. 

Given the prior session failed too, and the environment is stripped, let me take the most reliable engineering path:

**Use Puppeteer (npm) directly to drive a real Chromium that bypasses Cloudflare, extract all tokens/HTML/CSS/fonts, save to refs. Then build the clone from the captured snapshot.**

The builder worker can run node scripts with puppeteer to:
- Launch chromium with a realistic user agent
- Navigate to autodoc.es, wait for Cloudflare to clear
- Extract full HTML, computed styles, fonts, assets
- Screenshot at multiple viewports

This sidesteps the AdaL browser-use backend entirely. The worker uses `bash` (which works) to run puppeteer scripts.

But will Cloudflare clear under puppeteer's headless chromium? Cloudflare's bot management detects headless chromium. This is the hard part. Options:
- puppeteer-extra-plugin-stealth
- Use a non-headless (headed) chromium with xvfb
- Set realistic UA + headers

This is getting complex but it's the realistic path. However, I'm spending a lot of effort. Let me step back and reconsider whether the task is even achievable, and whether I should escalate to the user.

Actually — the right move per my role: this is a significant blocker (no browser backend, Cloudflare bot protection, stripped environment, prior session failed). Per "Surface only key decisions... Confirm when the plan CHANGES." This is a blocker that changes feasibility. 

But before escalating, let me make ONE solid attempt to get a browser working, since that's the crux. Let me:
1. Check the changelog for browser-use setup specifics
2. Try installing chromium via puppeteer (it bundles a known-good chromium) — this is the fastest way to get a real browser binary
3. Test if it can even reach autodoc.es past Cloudflare with stealth

Actually, let me reconsider the AdaL browser-use backend. Let me check if maybe browser-use mode requires running `adal` with some setup, or if there's a config. Let me look at the changelog entries and grep the backend's _internal for any browser-related python modules, and check if maybe playwright gets pip-installed into a writable location on first browser-use launch.

Let me do a focused diagnostic batch.

metadata.raw_response_str:
No system chromium, no Python, but npm registry is reachable and there's 941GB free. The AdaL backend is a frozen binary with no Playwright bundled. Before committing to a workaround, I need to know exactly how AdaL's browser-use mode expects to find a browser — let me check the changelog and whether first-use auto-installs anything. <tools_1fec>[{"name": "bash", "kwargs": {"command": <raw_1fec>CHANGELOG=/usr/local/lib/node_modules/@sylphai/adal-cli/node_modules/@sylphai/adal-cli-linux-x64/CHANGELOG.md
echo "=== changelog: browser-use / playwright / install / chromium context ===" 
grep -niE "browser|playwright|chromium|ms-playwright|install" "$CHANGELOG" 2>/dev/null | head -40
echo "=== _internal python packages list (look for playwright/browser-use) ==="
ls /usr/local/lib/node_modules/@sylphai/adal-cli/node_modules/@sylphai/adal-cli-linux-x64/backend/adal-backend/_internal/ 2>/dev/null | grep -iE "playwright|browser|chrome|patchright|nodriver|selenium|cdp|camoufox" 
echo "=== full _internal listing (top) ==="
ls /usr/local/lib/node_modules/@sylphai/adal-cli/node_modules/@sylphai/adal-cli-linux-x64/backend/adal-backend/_internal/ 2>/dev/null | head -60
echo "=== does adal have a setup/install subcommand or browser config? ==="
adal plugin list 2>&1 | head -20
echo "=== adal config / browser env docs ==="
ls /root/.adal 2>/dev/null
echo "=== look for browser config in ~/.adal ==="
find /root/.adal -maxdepth 2 -type f 2>/dev/null | head -20</raw_1fec>, "skip_approval": true}}]</tools_1fec>

metadata.thinking:
Let me look at the settings.json and check the prior engineer transcript for what was attempted. The key question is: does AdaL browser-use mode expect Playwright installed, and can it auto-download?

Let me check settings.json and the most relevant part of the prior transcript — how they tried to solve the 403/cloudflare.

Actually, I'm spending a lot of effort. Let me be strategic. The user wants the autodoc.es clone. Two real blockers:
1. No browser in container (Playwright/Chromium missing)
2. Cloudflare bot protection on autodoc.es

Let me check the prior transcript more carefully for whether they EVER got past cloudflare, and check settings.json for browser config. Let me also try installing playwright via npm (the npm registry is reachable). Actually — the adal backend is a frozen binary; it won't use an npm-installed playwright. But maybe browser-use mode spawns playwright via the python backend which looks for playwright in standard locations.

Let me look at the settings and a focused chunk of the prior transcript.

## AUTODOC: todo para su coche

Le damos la bienvenida a AUTODOC, el principal minorista online de piezas de coche y accesorios para vehículos de Europa. Desde nuestra fundación en 2008, solo tenemos un objetivo: que el mantenimiento de su coche le resulte lo más fácil posible. Hoy en día, más de 30 millones de clientes satisfechos en 27 países diferentes dan fe del éxito de esta visión original.

Previous

### Nuestra oferta

Piezas imprescindibles y accesorios completos para su coche

*   7,8 millones de productos
*   Más de 2700 fabricantes de marcas, como ATE, Bosch, Continental, Hella, Meyle, Sachs y TRW
*   Búsqueda de piezas eficaz por matrícula del vehículo y 85 000 configuraciones de modelos de vehículos
*   25 000 guías y tutoriales para realizar el mantenimiento y reparaciones por su cuenta
*   Asistencia profesional para buscar piezas y comprobar su compatibilidad
*   Opción de ampliar el plazo de devolución a 200 días
*   Planificación del mantenimiento mediante la app

### Nuestros servicios

![Image 291: Atención al cliente de AUTODOC](https://cdn.autodoc.de/uploads/info_section/article/4006/1778150779_27_92a1bb670653230fd95d2899f8559ec5.png)

Asistencia personalizada para el mantenimiento y las reparaciones de su coche

El equipo de atención al cliente de AUTODOC está disponible en 25 idiomas. Además de ofrecer asistencia general, el equipo, formado por mecánicos expertos en vehículos, también está a su disposición para ayudarle a encontrar las piezas adecuadas para su coche. Mediante la app de AUTODOC CLUB, puede planificar el mantenimiento de su vehículo e intercambiar ideas con otros amantes de los coches. Pero eso no es todo: si prefiere tomar cartas en el asunto, podemos ayudarle gracias a las más de 20 000 guías en PDF y a unos 4000 tutoriales en vídeo sobre cuestiones de mantenimiento y reparaciones.

![Image 292: Aplicación AUTODOC](https://cdn.autodoc.de/uploads/info_section/article/4006/1778150843_1422_9d804cfb06451d65952dc4568e357dc2.png)

### Nuestra gama de productos

Todo lo que se necesita para un viaje tranquilo

Ofrecemos una completa gama de piezas y accesorios para coche de fabricantes de marcas conocidas, así como de nuestras marcas propias de alto rendimiento RIDEX, RIDEX PLUS y RIDEX REMAN.

![Image 293: Oferta de piezas de AUTODOC](https://cdn.autodoc.de/uploads/info_section/article/4006/1778150793_1775_77c0e00af20e85df0c246b063d8e8d5c.png)

En nuestro surtido, encontrará piezas de recambio para coches, para vehículos híbridos y eléctricos con batería, piezas para camiones y motos, neumáticos y ruedas, así como aceites y líquidos de motor, herramientas y otros accesorios para coches.

Nuestra gama de 7,8 millones de piezas y accesorios para vehículos se divide en 38 categorías y varios cientos de subcategorías. Para facilitar una búsqueda rápida y eficaz de la pieza exacta que necesita para su coche, puede buscar directamente utilizando el número de matrícula de su vehículo. Además, disponemos de una base de datos con 85 000 configuraciones de modelos diferentes de 176 marcas de coches, 23 marcas de camiones y 154 marcas de motos. De este modo, nos aseguramos de encontrar exactamente la pieza de recambio que se adecúa a su vehículo.

### Nuestra infraestructura

![Image 294: La cadena de suministro de AUTODOC](https://cdn.autodoc.de/uploads/info_section/article/4006/1778150788_6621_784d153f5a9d0de0ff4a47cc31cdb44b.png)

Servicio especializado y plazos de entrega rápidos para los pedidos

Los más de 50 000 pedidos al día no se gestionan solos. En 2025, procesamos un total de 18,9 millones de pedidos de más de 9 millones de clientes activos, un hito que requiere experiencia, colaboración y una cadena de suministro bien engrasada. AUTODOC cuenta con:

*   Más de 5500 empleados en Alemania, Bélgica, Francia, Italia, Kazajistán, Luxemburgo, Moldavia, Países Bajos, Polonia, Portugal, Reino Unido, República Checa y Ucrania
*   Más de 200 proveedores de Europa y Asia
*   Capacidades logísticas en constante crecimiento: actualmente 4 centros logísticos en Alemania, Bélgica, Polonia y República Checa, así como otros 7 almacenes con una superficie total de 140 000 m²

Todo ello nos permite estar seguros de que la pieza de recambio que necesita llega de la forma más rápida y económica posible, tanto para coches como para vehículos eléctricos con batería, motos, camiones o utilitarios.

### Nuestra fiabilidad

Seguridad, protección y diligencia para clientes y vehículos

Como empresa de gran envergadura, nos comprometemos con nuestros clientes, así como con nuestros colaboradores comerciales, nuestros proveedores, los países en los que desarrollamos nuestra actividad y el mundo en el que vivimos.

Nuestros productos cumplen todas las normas necesarias, desde las normas ISO internacionales y las directrices de la UE hasta las respectivas obligaciones de etiquetado y eliminación a nivel local.

La protección y la seguridad de los datos son algo esencial para nosotros. AUTODOC trata los datos de carácter personal de forma transparente y en estricto cumplimiento del marco legal vigente. En cuanto sus datos no sean necesarios, serán eliminados o anonimizados.

![Image 295: Seguridad de los datos de los clientes](https://cdn.autodoc.de/uploads/info_section/article/4006/1778150779_2622_80c27686f1c6513013cac8e2a4435a3d.png)

AUTODOC cumple estrictamente todos los requisitos legales relativos al derecho de devolución y la garantía. Además de un plazo de devolución habitual de 14 días, también ofrecemos la posibilidad de ampliar dicho plazo a 200 días mediante la opción Pedido seguro. Las piezas de recambio que pida en nuestra tienda online están sujetas a la garantía establecida por ley.

### Nuestra responsabilidad

Innovación y sostenibilidad para todo el sector

Una industria del automóvil duradera exige innovación y sostenibilidad. Al fomentar las reparaciones, el mantenimiento y las mejoras apropiadas, favorecemos un uso más prolongado de los vehículos. En el ámbito de la sostenibilidad, AUTODOC ha definido unos objetivos claros:

*   Acción climática en toda la cadena de valor
*   Soluciones inteligentes para el embalaje y la eliminación de residuos
*   Cultura del trabajo justa e inclusiva
*   Integridad del producto y confianza del cliente

Nuestra marca de piezas de coche remanufacturadas RIDEX REMAN es solo un ejemplo de cómo contribuimos a fomentar la sostenibilidad dentro del mercado de piezas de recambio y de cómo cumplimos nuestra responsabilidad social a través del reciclaje y la remanufacturación.

Mediante el cumplimiento de los requisitos actuales para un gobierno corporativo sostenible y gracias a nuestros principios éticos, transparencia y responsabilidades, constituimos una base para moldear de forma activa el progreso en el ámbito de la sostenibilidad, no solo para acompañarlo.

Asimismo, AUTODOC participa con regularidad en actividades de patrocinio tanto dentro de la industria del automóvil como fuera de ella, y también apoya causas benéficas para cumplir con nuestra responsabilidad social.

### Nuestro reconocimiento

Satisfacción del cliente, excelencia e innovación demostradas

Nos enorgullecemos de que nuestro rendimiento y éxito queden reflejados en los premios recibidos por parte de nuestros clientes y nuestro sector:

*   Tres veces ganador del premio a mejor tienda online alemana en la categoría Piezas/Accesorios de coche (2024, 2025, 2026; DISQ/ntv)
*   Premio Motor Vehicles and Mobility concedido por clientes alemanes en 2024 (DtGV)

Movilidad: sencilla, transparente, sostenible y económica

Uso de cookies y de tecnologías similares

Esta página web utiliza cookies que son necesarias para el funcionamiento de la misma. Con el consentimiento otorgado por su parte, otras cookies y tecnologías similares ("herramientas") son utilizadas, en particular, para la integración de contenidos externos, el análisis de uso, la personalización de ofertas y la mejora de la funcionalidad de la página web.

Puede utilizar los botones que aparecen a continuación para aceptar o rechazar todas estas herramientas. En "Gestionar preferencias", podrá encontrar información detallada sobre todas las herramientas y ajustar sus configuraciones individualmente. Si, al utilizar una herramienta, los datos fueran transferidos a terceros países fuera del EEE cuyo nivel de protección de datos no fuera lo suficientemente adecuado, su consentimiento abarca expresamente esta transferencia y el tratamiento posterior de dichos datos.

Puede revocar su consentimiento o actualizar su selección en cualquier momento mediante la opción "Configuración de cookies" que encontrará en el pie de página de nuestra página web.

Para obtener más información, le rogamos que consulte nuestra  Política de privacidad.

 Herramientas de análisis 

Las herramientas de análisis se emplean para la recopilación estadística y el análisis del comportamiento del usuario, para lo cual son evaluados los datos de acceso durante su visita. En este sentido, analizamos por ejemplo los términos de búsqueda introducidos, la frecuencia de las visitas a las páginas o el uso de determinadas funciones de la página web. Con la ayuda de esta evaluación, podemos optimizar nuestra página web y mejorar su experiencia de usuario.

 Herramientas de marketing 

Las herramientas de marketing se emplean para proporcionar publicidad personalizada o recomendar productos interesantes ayudándose de los perfiles de los usuarios. Los datos de acceso son evaluados con el fin de mostrar al usuario publicidad, en nuestra página web y en las páginas de otros proveedores, que se corresponda con sus intereses y necesidades reales. Para hacer esto posible, también hacemos uso del retargeting y medimos la tasa de éxito (conversión) de nuestras campañas publicitarias. Entre las herramientas de marketing también se incluyen el inicio de sesión con cuentas de redes sociales, plugins de redes sociales y medios externos integrados, tales como vídeos, mapas y sistemas de valoración.
